[BEST EVIDENCE CHUNK]: A photograph of an illuminated igloo is often shared on social media along with the claim that "this is what an igloo looks like when you build a fire inside": 
 This is a genuine photograph of an illuminated igloo. However, the light source is not a fire built inside this snowy structure. It's actually a flashlight. 
 This picture was snapped by Ukrainian photographer Volodymyr Shevchuk in the Carpathian Mountains circa 2015. While we couldn't find this exact image on Shevchuk's social media profiles, he has posted several other photographs of this illuminated igloo to both his Instagram and Facebook pages:

[BEST EVIDENCE CHUNK]: We've fallen down an amusing internet rabbit hole full of hilarious photos that at first glance make no sense at all - in a totally hilarious way. 
 The internet is full of wonderful and amusing images, from daft memes to amusing pet photographs, but this is on a whole other level. You're going to see all sorts of chortle worthy nonsense here that'll either have you scratching your head or having a good chortle. 
 If you enjoy these, we'd recommend following this Twitter account for more in future. 
 Igloo with a glow 
 You might be wondering why there's a bright glow coming from inside this Igloo. Well, it's from a fire built within which melts the inside layer of ice. This process then allows for a refreezing which in turn adds an extra layer of insulation. That can then help the Igloo be a warm 15 degrees centigrade on the inside while it's well below freezing outside.
